programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers head to Bowes Museum, taking in the wonders of the Teesdale countryside along the way. With exclusive access to the museum, the family bring a mechanical swan back to life, before reliving fond family memories at High Force waterfall.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Donna and Paul dabble with DIY danger as they tackle a risky utility room. Debbie and Dave overhaul a barn to launch a craft business. Tim and Krys revive a bland reception.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil look back on their best budget-stretching renovations, from a cramped three-bed semi in Long Eaton to a not-so-done-up terrace in Shropshire.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first day of the competition in Weston-super-Mare, Zumba teacher Amanda is determined to win her guests over with her locally sourced Somerset menu, including pork in cider. Her night kicks off with some friendly banter until young upstart Harry tries to guess everyones ages.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Weston-super-Mare, rugby-mad sports centre manager Harry hopes to impress his guests with a menu of Hungarian classics, from beef in hunters sauce to birds milk with meringue dumplings. But drama teacher Nancy is out for revenge.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Weston-super-Mare, drama teacher and music lover Nancy throws an Austrian-themed party to reflect her love of the country and its music. Her menu includes Austrian specialities including Wiener schnitzel and venison stew. Her night starts with hilarity when rugby player Harry thinks the clothes he found on the house tour are fancy dress costumes.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Weston-super-Mare, farm estate manager Chris hosts an evening based on his love of all things African, with savoury donuts, deep fried beef and exotic dessert. Chriss donuts are a new taste experience for most of his guests, but go down well.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businesswoman Tracy hosts the final dinner party in Weston-super-Mare. Tracy has designed her evening around her love of dance, from the tango to the cha cha cha. Shes asked her guests to wear black tie and ballgowns, and for entertainment theyll get a dance lesson from Tracy.

programme.title: George Clarkes Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George meets the man trying to build the first UFO-styled pre-fabricated Futuro house seen in this country for half a century, but with the kit in 16 pieces, he struggles to bring this retro vision of the future back to life.

Ajay and his mom agree to do so.

Convincing him to stay alive and then move in with him, Ram helps Ravi find a job, which in turn finds him a wife. programme.desc: Mitch and his lifeguard team rush to save Hobie, ex-wife Gayle and her fiance after their plane crashes in the water.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Romance prevails when Jackie tries to intensify her relationship with Mitch, Matt and Summer deal with their feelings for each other and Hobie falls in love.

programme.title: Baywatch

programme.desc: Mitch agonizes over the near-drowning of a boy who lies in a coma.

programme.title: Baywatch

programme.desc: Mitch and the Baywatch lifeguards are asked by gymnast Mary Lou Retton to help organize an athletic competition for developmentally challenged youths.

programme.desc: Matt is asked for help by a former love, motorcycle racer Jesse Majors, when her bosss romantic interest in her becomes obsessive.

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras
programme.desc: Tha Stella ag iarraidh a bhith àbhaisteach ach tha beachd eile aig Màthair na Bànrigh! Stella wants to be like everyone else but the Queen Mother has other ideas. She would like to ensure that Stella isnt normal.

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras

programme.title: Scams and Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.desc: Claudia looks back at some of the highlights from the series, featuring her studio audience and an array of star-studded sofa guests, including Lisa Kudrow, Jennifer Saunders, Niall Horan, Guz Khan, Jeff Goldblum, James McAvoy, Rita Wilson, Jamie Dornan, Anna Faris, Jimmy Carr, Ralph Fiennes, Rachel Zegler, Jamie Dornan and many more.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Scams and Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: Scams and Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens

programme.desc: Stacey heads to Bedfordshire to the private site where 23-year-old Chantelle lives with her husband, Swayley. Chantelle prides herself in keeping with the old Gypsy values her granny Rita taught her and sharing them on social media.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: In Britain the shoplifting is at an all-time high, incidents of violence and abuse against shop staff have skyrocketed, and a third of Brits have admitted to stealing through the self-checkouts. To understand whats causing this high-street epidemic, Stacey is meeting the people behind the crime and spending time on the shop floor with the staff who deal with shoplifting every day. In Manchester city centre, beauty and cosmetics chain Bodycare has one of its biggest and most shoplifted stores.

programme.title: Ghosts

Alison promises that theyll be back by the evening and whats more, reveals to the ghosts that there is a Christmas surprise for each of them in the library.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Its coming up to Christmas, and Mikes mum is staying. Betty quickly overstays her welcome, but Alison and Mike dont quite have the heart to tell her. Just as she decides to leave of her own volition, Betty suspects something is terribly wrong.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda isnt your average girly girl. At 6 ft 1, Miranda gets called Sir once too often, much to the amusement of her childhood friend Stevie who manages Mirandas joke shop. Miranda bumps into her old friend Gary, who is working in the restaurant next door as the new chef. When he asks her out for a drink she gets over excited by the prospect of her first real date and decides its time to try to be more girly.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda is rather British when it comes to sex, or shenanigans as she likes to call it. Trying to overcome her hang-ups she decides the way forward with Gary would be to create a romantic moment so hell see her in a sexual light. Meanwhile, Stevie persuades her to join an evening class to learn French, the language of love.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Actor Richard returns home from London to visit his dad Ken, a year on from the passing of his mum Jean. But on arriving, he realises his dad may need more help than hed originally thought.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard decides the time has come to clear Kens house of all his junk or as Ken calls it, his memories. As Kens suspicions over Richards true motives grow, the arrival of Mr Oddjob and Richards former school nemesis, complicates matters further.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard offers to take Ken to the local golf club, unaware that hes about to play a much bigger part in integrating his dad back into the community than hed originally intended.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard battles medical receptionists in order to secure Ken a doctors appointment and is asked to impart his acting wisdom to the local am-dram group.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard secures an audition and endeavours to track down Kens oldest friend, who Ken lost touch with years ago, in order to invite him to Kens surprise 75th birthday party.

programme.title: Only Child

programme.sub-title: Traditional Stone Carving and Stained Glass
programme.desc: Dom revisits stone carver Louise and stained-glass artist Charlotte to find out how they have built thriving businesses and how much they earn after leaving his business bootcamp.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion time travels back to the 70s in Nottinghamshire, and it all feels very familiar. Martin is loving his surroundings in Bath, and Jacqui sees beyond the work that needs to be done in Norfolk to appreciate the potential.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Bristol, distillery manager Luke is relying on caffeine to keep stress at bay - but Clive Myrie has a gentler, smarter swap in mind. If Luke traded his coffee and energy drinks for tea, the benefits could be huge. Studies show that drinking black tea regularly can reduce anxiety, calm the brain and even support heart health. A simple brew with surprisingly powerful perks.

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Kerry is known as The Kingdom in Ireland, and its Ronans next stop on his Wild Atlantic journey. He joins his big brother Gary on the stunning Lough Leane in Killarney for a fishing trip, one that they first went on together 40 years ago. He also meets a couple who make traditional handcrafted leather items and gets to work on a guitar strap, before running onto the pitch at Dr Crokes GAA club to play some hurling, the fastest field sport in the world.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `cucumber, and goes on a whirlwind word adventure. See cucumbers that are crunchy, mushy in the middle and curly. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dees friend finally succeeds in saying todays word.

programme.desc: he Twirlywoos are in a kitchen, where they watch a man turn on a tap and fill a glass with water. When he leaves the Twirlywoos come out and turn the tap on. They fill a glass with water and then start to find other things around the kitchen to fill up.

programme.title: Something Special

programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Remi, George, Zadie, Idris, Felicity, Tommy and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside everyones bags in this episode are cardboard boxes which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding baby appropriate cardboard boxes inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The fun song `The Wheels on the Bus! features, as does a story involving Baby Bear.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy

programme.title: Whats in Your Plant Pot?

Finally, the Recycling Express inspires Murphy to help Rebecca turn different-sized cardboard boxes into a cake.

programme.title: Vegesaurs

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: While camping in the bush, Bluey makes a new friend who doesnt speak the same language.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: As Roly makes his usual noise in the Clubhouse, Duggee and the Squirrels reminisce about how completely different Roly was when he first arrived. Hiding behind his dad, Roly wasnt loud at all but impossibly quiet. As Duggee shows Roly around the Clubhouse, he squeals, hides and hangs off his dad, and just seems altogether too shy to fit in. Duggee has a great idea to introduce Roly to Norrie, thinking a friend will help him to settle in.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Mrs Weaver returns with a song all about seasons. Its a celebration of spring, summer, autumn and winter.

programme.title: Our Family

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Kay has invited his friends to taste his Polish dill pickle soup, which his mum has shown him how to cook all by himself. Dill pickle is a popular Polish ingredient and Kay gets his friends to taste it before they eat the soup. Not all of his friends like the taste at first, but Kay hopes they will all enjoy the finished soup. As well as dill pickle, he uses spring onions, potatoes and carrot in his soup.

programme.title: Do You Know?

programme.title: Dog Squad

programme.title: Steve and Aneeshwar Go Wild
programme.desc: Steve and Aneeshwar meet some of the zoos acrobats, a family of fossa who are incredible climbers and enjoy jumping high up in the trees. Next, they go to see the leaf-tailed gecko, which has an amazing tail that looks just like a leaf.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle is unwell. Hes in his pyjamas and under strict orders to stay in bed and rest. But poor Waffle cant get comfy. When he sees Evie and Simon playing in the garden, he feels left out and begins to woof and woof. Simon tells Waffle to rest quietly, but its too late. The wonder dogs incessant barking has already disturbed Mrs Hobbs next door and, to make matters worse, like Waffle shes not feeling well either.

programme.desc: Its the day the geese fly over on their annual migration south. When Sonny reports that geese put on an amazing display if you mimic their calls, the gang look for the right sound to get a response from the geese before they fly past.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: With only minutes to go before the big performance at McGintys farm, Maddie has to use all her musical skills to convince Gary the Chicken to perform with the other animals.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels accidentally break the Chickens TV ariel during the season finale of their favourite soap opera. How will they see the finale now.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The Numberblocks are exploring Fraction Island and meet new Fraction Friends on the way!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The Alphablocks have fun naming their hens and, finally, their helper, BEN the pen!

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends learn all about getting their eyes tested. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble helps Grandad Tumble with some new glasses when he loses his old pair.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies have fun moving backwards and forwards. The Tubby Custard Ride goes forwards, then backwards, then forwards again.

programme.title: Ranger Hamzas Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how grasses play such an important role in the environment. They find out that grasses can be food for animals and some can be turned into ingredients to make food for people too. On the way, they discover how long grass can help shelter small animals and insects, and that cut grass can be useful too, before finding a lovely grassy place to relax and have fun.

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Sophie must infiltrate the sports hall without disturbing a relaxation class Val is running to retrieve bibs, goals and a big bag of footballs.

programme.title: Graces Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that all move because of propellers. Grace flies a seaplane, shows off unusual hydrocopter and goes for a fast ride in a raceboat.

programme.title: Dodges Pup School
programme.desc: An unexpected visitor has arrived in the playground, a cat. The pups are initially scared, after all theyve heard that cats have lasers for eyes. But Dodge decides to be brave and go and talk to it.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster is finding it hard to think of something new to do on Do Something New Day, so he heads to Tiniest Fluffiest Bunnys house to see if she has any ideas.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.sub-title: The Octonauts and the Golden Mole
programme.desc: Tracker and Kwazii swim through sand with a mole before a storm hits; Paani keeps a rat pack from snacking on endangered insects in New Zealand.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Tunnel Rumbler
programme.desc: When Peter and his friends mistake the echoey rebbits of a lost Jeremy Fisher for a scary monster, they set about to rid their tunnels of this mysterious invader. But with Tommy Brock on the warpath nearby, it wont be easy!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are trying to count how many badges Duggee has when they come across his Counting Badge. He got it for counting to 100.This sounds like hard work.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Zigzags ADHD gives her a case of the hops, so Vida teaches her how to make a to-do list.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea sends a load of Mini Peas to over-run and takeover Tato Tower. Theres only one person who can help him - the expert pea-yodeller, Prickly Pear.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: The Ducklings throw a no adults party, so Weasy parties solo but makes a huge mess.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo are great at picking apples with Mojos Crane Top. But Prestons drone might be even better until it accidentally scoops up Felix. Mojo and Bo are on the chase.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey is unable to get to sleep, so Mum reads her one last story. Unicorse, the most annoying hand puppet in the world, interrupts them, and Bluey tries to reform it.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Exhausted with requests for help, Looph deliberately disappears. The Piris turn detectives, searching the berg with little success until her ingenious plan is finally revealed.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Mum and Dad have a disagreement, Bluey makes Dad write a love letter to Mum to make up. Now Bluey must find a way to deliver the letter to Mum.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: PC Plum jumps to conclusions when he hears about an upcoming volcanic eruption. But little does he know, its Avas science project thats set to fizzle in a safe experiment.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Essam family visit the Brooklyn-Bells. Jess hopes their well-behaved dog, Baxter, will teach Waffle how to be good, but Waffle has other ideas.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: A wayward camping trip teaches Rafi that changing the plan can lead to adventure.

programme.title: JoJo and Gran Gran
programme.desc: Gran Grans keys get stuck behind the sofa. Inspired by inventors from the past, JoJo and Gran Gran invent a `key collector to get them back.

programme.title: Sarah and Duck
programme.desc: An excited Sarah and Duck take the Shallots to see the Ribbon Sisters perform with their family circus.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Major Clanger doesnt hear the other Clangers calling him for soup. Tiny and Small fetch him but he decides he must invent a new system so that this wont happen again.

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa discover a dandelion clock and have fun blowing the seeds. The Yummies help them plant the seeds, and they marvel at how a seed can become a beautiful flower.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos are playing their music so fast that everybody does everything very fast too. Igglepiggle and Upsy Daisy dance, and Makka Pakka washes stones until at last, the Tombliboos slow down.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.sub-title: P-T: Jade Thirlwall: The Dress in the Window
programme.desc: Jade Thirlwall reads a beautiful story about a little boy who dreams of a special outfit. The Dress in the Window is by Robert Tregoning and illustrated by Pippa Curnick.

programme.desc: Vinod Joshi lives a middle-class existence with his wife, Meena, and a young son. Their well being is shattered by the arrival of a letter from an ex-employee, alleging that he saw Vinod committing a murder. Meena initially dismisses it as absurd, but one day Vinod returns home in a bloodied state telling there has been a homicide. She attempts to trace the origin of this wealth and gets more and more convinced about his guilt.

All before Barney treats his dad to an exclusive performance with the Polish National Radio Symphony Orchestra in honour of Polands most famous composer, Chopin.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike returns Elvis to his F1 roots with a car from one of the most famous names in British racing, Lotus. The Europa, in fact, reckoned to be the first mass-produced mid-engine sports car. Elvis is excited -- until he takes the car out for a spin.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Gold Divers

programme.title: Moonshiners
programme.desc: After Mike asks the wrong shiner for help, Mark and Digger land a skilled new partner. On a mountain run, Mark and Huck nearly meet the barrel of a gun. Jerry attempts a comeback as a land sale forces him and Mike to abandon their column still site.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: R n B singer Keyshia Cole is literally dreaming of a beautiful saltwater aquarium in her home. Luckily for ATM, her social media followers led her to them and Wayde and Brett want to make her dreams come true.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: A California restaurant wants a tank for their new location in Santa Monica. The owners want a nostalgic feel and replicas to represent its 70-year history while pointing to the restaurants future.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Donut Mania is Bretts newest business venture and they are having a grand opening. Wayde and the whole team want to congratulate him on his success with a surprise fish tank for the new business.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Businessman Brad Jennings has built a new office, now he wants to complete it and finish it off with three tanks. The guys work on three complementary interiors which include a working fireplace.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: World champion boxer, Fernando Vargas, asks ATM to make a boxing glove-shaped tank for his boxing gym in Las Vegas. This challenge goes way beyond the ring as the team attempt to deliver a knockout.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Howie Mandel wants the team to build another tank. This time it is a surprise for his managers company and their an existing tank needs a seriously major upgrade.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Filthy Fortunes
programme.desc: A New York City home features `Back to the Future self-lacing sneakers worth up to $100,000, mint-condition `Knight Rider toys and hip-hop tour jackets. The team also cleans a property covered in scrap metal to hunt for rare Olympics memorabilia.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase Morrill and his team tackle the renovation of a 130-year-old cabin on an island. With a budget of $40,000 and six weeks, can they overcome the challenges of building on an island to ensure the cabin lasts another 100 years?

programme.title: Naked and Afraid
programme.desc: Two complete strangers choose to take on the Everest of survival challenges. Theyll have no water, food, or clothes, but they must find a way to work together and survive in the wilderness for 21 days -- Naked and Afraid.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: Countdown of NASAs top six the deadliest stars - vampire stars that drain their neighbours, supernovas and white dwarfs that destroy planets.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: Despite Plutos appearance as a dark and frozen world, scientists are investigating whether there might be warm liquid water beneath the surface.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Memphis

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Memphis
programme.desc: JJ, Lee Roberts and Jackie look to keep the MSO alive in the competition.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: When Josh Gates receives new, potential video proof of a giant creature living in Lake Champlain, he sends Phil and Jess to investigate the 30-foot serpent thats been spotted in the lake for generations.

programme.desc: Josh treks deep into Siberia for a site where priceless mammoth bones are mined out of the permafrost. Once he gets back to the lab, Josh views the DNA results to find out whether the woolly mammoth will walk the Earth again.

programme.desc: With one kid already in college, Adina and Trey are looking to downsize. They are eager to sell fast before a subdivision floods the neighbourhood with a thousand brand new homes, but their outdated property is making a lousy first impression.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The co-owners of South Brooklyn Foundry dont see eye-to-eye, causing communication to grow toxic while the restaurant falls into more debt; Gordon Ramsay steps in to reassess the Brooklyn eaterys priorities and straighten out the finances.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Dara Ó Briain finds himself baffled by a quantity of milk, John Kearns does some elaborate skipping, Munya Chawawa does some elaborate jumping, and Fern Brady walks too slowly. Amid the carnage, Little Alex Horne finds himself largely ignored.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Sean Lock and Jon Richardson once again take on the famous words and numbers quiz. Jimmy Carr hosts proceedings, and Sean Lock faces Jon Richardson in a rematch of the original encounter. David ODoherty is in Dictionary Corner along with Countdown regular Susie Dent. Meanwhile the countrys favourite boffin Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers with a little help from comedian Joe Wilkinson.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Rhod Gilbert take on Lee Mack and Rob Beckett. Tim Key is in `Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, and Rachel Riley is assisted by Joe Wilkinson.

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: The team face their first test during Reynholm Industries so-called `war on stress. The office is on fire, Roy has a sudden attack of kleptomania, and Jens new shoes are two sizes too small.

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: Jen has a new admirer, but his romantic gesture goes horribly wrong when Jens inability to tell the truth results in a very public humiliation. Meanwhile, Moss and Roy conduct an experiment with a `lonely hearts website.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Extreme
programme.desc: Sketch gives Andy a hand with his disastrous digits, while Alice designs popstar Michelle Heaton something a little special. Also, Pash rids Carly of her cracking calamity and Uzzi works on Ians booby blunder.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Manchester, Carl and his German wife Anja serve up a meal inspired by Anjas homeland, while Leroy and Hayley cook Caribbean. However, Sangeeta and Sreekanth try to dance their way to the prize.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Bedfordshire, Tom and Naomi prepare posh nosh, Victoria and Elliot serve a controversial goat curry and Neil and Charlie plan to impress their guests with mystery and magic.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Lee has just three weeks and £12,000 to pull off the perfect day for his bride-to-be Ellie. Lee wants a huge wedding that celebrates his dwarfism, and shows off his sense of humour. However, all Ellie wants is a low key, elegant and intimate wedding.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: All Hannah wants is a luxurious and relaxing wedding day where she looks a million dollars, but Tommy has a big romantic plan up his sleeve for his ex-water-skiing champ bride.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Petrified Bride
programme.desc: The skys the limit for adrenaline junkie Jai as he plans on having a wedding in the sky, neglecting the fact that his bride-to-be Leanne is terrified of heights.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Party animal Harry has three weeks and £12,000 to pull off a day to impress his perfectionist bride-to-be Rosa, but while she dreams of a stylish, vintage English wedding thats all about romance and tradition. Harry, however, would like a fun full-scale Bollywood-inspired extravaganza.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Fashion Show
programme.desc: Jenna dreams of a traditional and timeless wedding that will never go out of style, James wants a cutting-edge, catwalk fashion show ceremony full of the latest trends.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Steven Newman, CEO of Loehmanns department stores, goes undercover posing as an aspiring entrepreneur in a reality TV contest who is trying to win money to start up his own firm.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Jane Grote Abell, chairwoman of Donatos Pizza and daughter of the companys founder, goes undercover posing as a contestant on a reality TV show who is trying to win money to open up her own restaurant.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay goes back to three restaurants - Handlebar, Casa Roma and The Black Pearl - to check in with the owners he helped in the past and find out whether his advice has been put to good use.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The opening episode of a new series sees Gordon in Plainfield, New Jersey, trying to save a floundering soul food restaurant called Blackberrys. He comes up against the indomitable Shelley, an owner who runs her establishment with an iron fist, and doesnt want to hear what Gordon has to say. Gordon must find a way to convince her to change her ways if the restaurant is going to stand a chance.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across the United States, travelling to New Jersey to try to snatch Italian restaurant Leones from the jaws of failure. Owner Leone had a thriving business, but after a sudden illness she spent two years in hospital in a coma. When she returned, she was heartbroken to discover that her lazy son Michael had run all her efforts and the restaurant into the ground.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the first of an explosive two-part special, Gordon tries to turn around the fortunes of Los Angeles restaurant, the Burger Kitchen. Its only been open for 16 months but has already changed its menu more than 10 times, as well as going through 10 chefs and 10 waitresses, and owner Alan has spent his sons inheritance on the restaurant without permission, leading to much family conflict.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The explosive drama continues as Gordon is still in Los Angeles struggling to bring together the fragmented Saffron family and their failing restaurant, the Burger Kitchen. Last time, son Daniel nearly came to blows with the head chef, and Gordon was shocked to learn that dad Alan had taken from Daniels inheritance, without his knowledge, to help fund the start-up of the restaurant.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host the comedically contorted Countdown mash-up as Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont take on Rob Brydon and Joe Wilkinson, with Huge Davies in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Bewilderness
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Bill Bailey performs live at the Grand Theatre in Swansea and delivers hilariously unfocused and scattered comedy styles, as well as his enviable musical skills.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Its July 12th and the Quinn family want to get out of Derry before the Orange Parades start. Aunt Sarahs tarot cards predict a disaster for this trip - and she might be right.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Erin is over the moon when she becomes editor of the school magazine. Orlas obsessed by step aerobics and Da Gery is in trouble with Ma Mary, and theres romance for Aunt Sarah.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Stand-up comic and presenter Jimmy Carr is at the helm as comedians Sean Lock and Jon Richardson head up the teams for Christmas capers. programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Nathan loves music and Stoke-on-Trent, so when bride-to-be Beccie gives him free rein to plan their wedding, he honours them with nuptials based on Northern Soul.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay reluctantly takes Phil on a model-airplane excursion, and Gloria goes shopping with Alex. Meanwhile, Claire and Manny get to know one another as siblings.

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short clip from Rude Tubes naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring food and animals.

programme.desc: While Phil spends some quality time with Haley, taking her on a college tour of his old alma mater, Gloria and Jay are forced to have `the talk with Manny.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Broken Arrow
programme.desc: Set in Arizona in 1870, gold prospector Tom Jeffords is tired of the repetitive reprisal killings between settlers and the indigenous locals and plans a visit to the Apache leader, Cochise, to try and arrange a truce. After first being welcomed into the fold by Cochise, and even marrying the chiefs daughter, old racial problems resurface.

programme.title: Gun Fury
programme.desc: Wild west tale about a ranchers search for a ruthless and violent outlaw who has kidnapped his fiance.

programme.title: Siege of the Saxons
programme.desc: Upon the death of King Arthur, his daughter wins the right of selecting the knight she wants as a husband and heir.

programme.title: Cleopatra
programme.desc: Cleopatra uses both her political wiles and her body to first woo Julius Caesar and then Marc Antony to protect and even expand her realm. As history tells us, she was to meet an untimely end.

programme.title: Anna
programme.desc: Beneath a Russian womans striking beauty lies a hidden secret that will unleash her indelible strength and skill and lead to her becoming one of the most feared assassins on the planet.

programme.title: Alita: Battle Angel
programme.desc: Set several centuries in the future, the abandoned Alita is found in the scrapyard of Iron City by Ido, a compassionate cyber-doctor who takes the unconscious cyborg Alita to his clinic. When Alita awakens, she has no memory of who she is, nor does she have any recognition of the world she finds herself in. As Alita learns to navigate her new life and the treacherous streets of Iron City, Ido tries to shield her from her mysterious past.

programme.title: Sheroes
programme.desc: Four friends find themselves in over their heads when one of them gets kidnapped by a notorious drug lord in Thailand. As they fight to stay alive and protect one another, they employ a unique set of skills in a heart-pumping battle for survival.

programme.title: Broken Arrow
programme.desc: Set in Arizona in 1870, gold prospector Tom Jeffords is tired of the repetitive reprisal killings between settlers and the indigenous locals and plans a visit to the Apache leader, Cochise, to try and arrange a truce. After first being welcomed into the fold by Cochise, and even marrying the chiefs daughter, old racial problems resurface.

programme.title: Gun Fury

programme.title: Cleopatra
programme.desc: Cleopatra uses both her political wiles and her body to first woo Julius Caesar and then Marc Antony to protect and even expand her realm. As history tells us, she was to meet an untimely end.

programme.title: Anna
programme.desc: Beneath a Russian womans striking beauty lies a hidden secret that will unleash her indelible strength and skill and lead to her becoming one of the most feared assassins on the planet.

programme.title: Alita: Battle Angel
programme.desc: Set several centuries in the future, the abandoned Alita is found in the scrapyard of Iron City by Ido, a compassionate cyber-doctor who takes the unconscious cyborg Alita to his clinic. When Alita awakens, she has no memory of who she is, nor does she have any recognition of the world she finds herself in. As Alita learns to navigate her new life and the treacherous streets of Iron City, Ido tries to shield her from her mysterious past.

programme.desc: James is back in Brittany where he first learnt about French food as a student, and hes joined by fellow chef Martin Blunos for a dawn raid on the fish and vegetable market in Vannes.

programme.title: James Martins French Road Trip
programme.desc: James welcomes fellow chef Martin Blunos to his old student stamping ground in Brittany. They park up their trailer near Vannes, where the fresh seafood in the old market catches their imagination. Loaded up with a fabulous trawl they head back to the campsite to cook up Pot Au Feu and cod cheeks in local beer batter with home made tartare sauce.

programme.title: James Martins French Road Trip

programme.desc: Yotams journey begins with a trip to an unusual open-air restaurant - set in an idyllic cove and managed by a hermit. He grills red mullet, accompanied by a lemon and celery salad. Yotam also finds a Jewish restaurant that serves their twist on Tunisian dishes and where he prepares succulent chicken and sweetcorn meatballs, served with a tangy lemon salsa.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman travels across the United States to find the countrys best traditional food, while undertaking tough eating challenges along the way.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a soul food spot in Washington, DC, a specialist hotdog place in Kansas City, and a seafood shack in Massachusetts.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a Mediterranean spot in Tennessee, a cafe in Minnesota with wild rice French toast, and has turkey in Kentucky.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb makes the trip to Tampa for the ultimate Cuban platter at Floridas oldest restaurant, a stop at a speakeasy with a secret and 20 minutes of cookie combat with the `Cookie Munchers Challenge.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webbs palate-expanding pilgrimage takes him to Orlando for a hunger-slaying sandwich, a famous footlong and an 8.5 pound team challenge so big that he calls in backup from hometown hero and heartthrob Joey Fatone.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: The team are like kids in a toy store literally, when they are asked to make an FAO Schwarz-themed cake. They also have a beach-themed cake to create.

programme.title: Cake Boss

programme.desc: Guy visits a Cajun joint for smoked tasso, a Napoletana pizza place in San Antonio, Texas, and a Greek diner in Denver, Colorado.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cooking for Jeffrey: Big Breakfast Buffet
programme.desc: Ina entertains her serious foodie friends Devon Fredericks and Eli Zabar with a breakfast bash. The pressure is on as Ina creates a buffet of potato pancetta frittata, overnight Belgian waffles and a fresh fruit platter.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Its a weekend of photos, fun and style as Inas friend, photographer Miguel comes to visit. For his arrival, she makes spicy hermit bars, followed by the next days lunch of courgette and goat cheese tart, and tomato carpaccio.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina shares her kitchen tips and professional strategies for chicken. Recipes include roasted chicken with radishes, chicken and spinach Waldorf salad, and truffled chicken liver mousse.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like A Pro: Weeknight Dinners
programme.desc: Inas got weeknight dinners covered, pro-style! Recipes include roasted salmon tacos, cider roasted pork tenderloin and five-minute lemon capellini.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Fearless Food
programme.desc: Ina tackles daunting dishes with easy techniques, from a perfectly risen souffle à lorange to overcoming pastry fears with her totally professional-looking Annas tomato tart. Plus, a foolproof caramel sauce.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Its eggs all the way in an incredible master class taught by Ina. Recipes include Caesar salad with blue cheese and bacon, a pavlova fruit tart and eggs Benedict with an easy hollandaise sauce.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver takes root vegetables to another level, makes scrumptious sticky BBQ chicken, rattles out oozy cheesy enchiladas and turns things upside down with a pineapple cake, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: The average price of the recipes in this episode is just £1.55 per portion. Jamie cooks up a glorious mothership roast side of salmon with all the trimmings. Then, then turns his leftover salmon into an incredible salmon filo pie.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: All the recipes in this episode cost under £1.90 per portion. Jamie cooks up a `mothership roast chicken with all the trimmings and turns the leftovers into chicken stew with dumplings.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by chef pal Nick Nairn. Theyre enjoying the luxury trip of a lifetime aboard The Royal Scotsman. James visits the kitchen aboard the train, where he cooks up a traditional duck a lorange. Nick visits a special bakery housed in an old prisoner of war camp and James uses the bread to make a tasty ham and cheese toastie in the grounds of historic Scone Palace. The day ends back aboard the Royal Scotsman, where James and Nick dine in style in the beautiful dining room.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James takes a trip to the stunning Cairngorms National Park, where hes joined by chef pal Nick Nairn. Amongst their adventures, they visit some friendly reindeer and brave the rapids on the River Spey, where they go whitewater rafting. James takes a quad bike through the ancient pine forests, where he meets up with a farmer and his special breed of Highland cattle, which then inspires him to cook a tasty Highland beef carpaccio.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers are back in Scotland, but this time, theyre in the beautiful region of Dumfries and Galloway. Its another place close to Dave and Sis hearts, a rural paradise with a glorious coastline that stretches for more than 200 miles.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers explore Lancashire. For Dave, the trip is an emotional homecoming, as he was born in the county. They start their trip in the 120 square miles of glorious beauty thats Morecambe Bay.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: For their next adventure in the west of Britain, the Bikers are exploring Merseyside and the Wirral, a place that holds a lot of childhood memories for both Dave and Si.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Bikers continue their journey by heading to north west Wales, a remote but beautiful part of the UK and home to lush, green pastures, towering peaks, breathtaking valleys and 250 miles of stunning coastline.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs arrive at the Palace of Westminster to cook for the Banquet of Great Britons. The chefs have been selected because their dishes showcase contemporary British cuisine and demonstrate the transformation in British food during the Queens historic reign.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for top spot are Danny Parker, head chef at Michelin starred `House of Tides in Newcastle, who is back to try his luck in the competition again after being knocked out in the regional heat last year following a tense tie-break. Hes taking on two newcomers: Dan Fletcher, protege of Phil Howard, and now head chef at `Fenchurch in the City of London, and David Coulson,who runs `Peace and Loaf in Newcastle, which boasts 2 AA rosettes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course the chefs are all aiming for 10s with their unique fish dishes. David is celebrating a personal connection to the brief with his `My Cod I Bet Me Maam Has Seen Some Trotters, a tribute to his mum who has worked as a chiropodist. Dans `For All Those Sleepless Nights pays tribute to nightshift workers from the NHS with some bold but classic flavours while Dannys `Not All Superheroes Wear Capes aims to give a taste of luxury to the hardworking staff.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dan pays tribute to an icon of modern nursing, Florence Nightingale, while Danny in inspired by the founder of the NHS, Aneurin Bevan. Davids dish is dedicated to the air ambulance staff who saved the life of his fiance, and he even takes a trip to meet one of them.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs last chance to impress and tensions are high. Dan takes a risk with an unusual combination of ewes milk and bergamot while Danny is determined to pull out all the stops with his complex dessert `Aunty Gwens Chocolate. David hopes to transform a childhood comfort classic into a show stopping dessert. Dan also takes a trip to meet Dr Ben Clarke who was working in the Royal London Hospital AandE during the London Bridge terror attack.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they serve their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Banquet champion Selin Kiazim, a rising star of the London scene renowned for her Turkish Cypriot fusion flavours, is back hoping to join the elite group of chefs who have made it to the banquet twice. She is up against James Cochran whos worked at some of Londons toughest kitchens including 2 Michelin starred `The Ledbury and Scott Goss from `The Twenty Six in Tunbridge Wells, renowned for his use of local Kent ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs do battle for the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen is tense. Lobster is catch of the day for James whose dish is Caribbean-inspired. Scott is attempting to elevate a classic fish pie, dedicated to his mum who worked as a pharmacist in the NHS for 23 years. And Selin is taking a risk with a complex three-part fish dish featuring sardines, scallops and langoustines, as a nod to the purported health benefits of a Mediterranean diet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scott is showcasing rare breed Tamworth pork to say thank you to the rare breed of people who work for the NHS. Selin is serving a complex duck dish using ingredients from all around the world to honour `Our Global NHS. And James is presenting five cuts of goat cooked in five different ways, for his bold dish `Under The Knife.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Selin puts her own unique twist on tea and toast. James creates a decadent coconut parfait accompanied by mango puree filled doughnuts for his dessert `Tree of Life inspired by the coconut palm tree, the NHS and both of their contributions to our health. Scott hopes his elevated gypsy tart `Honey Bee Good will secure him a place in the final.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their four-course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they re-make their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Vying to be crowned champion of Scotland are three talented newcomers to the GBM kitchen. Lorna McNee is a former Scottish Chef of the Year and is renowned for her technical precision which she has gained from working her entire career at 2 Michelin-starred restaurant Andrew Fairlie at Gleneagles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are all out to impress. The heat is increased as all three serve their unique takes on lobster. Ross is hoping to snatch victory with his dish `Lobster Aid. Lornas `Live Well, Live Very Well features only three elements, each of which she needs to deliver perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ross is hoping to impress with `Oh Deer Its Time For Your Penicillin, his tribute to Scottish scientist Alexander Fleming featuring an unusual yeast element as well as venison. Across the kitchen, Lorna, a former Game Chef of the Year, is also cooking venison in a bid to impress.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the last chance for the chefs to impress before one is sent home. Ross is attempting to elevate a comfort classic into a dish worthy of the banquet. Lornas cooking a personal tribute to the NHS staff who have cared for her during treatment for a rare blood condition, using unusual yuzu and tonka bean flavours. Once again Ben is serving an experimental dish with his `Dance for the Greater Good which includes a bees wax ice cream.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judging day and the two remaining chefs must impress the panel of Oliver Peyton, Andi Oliver and Matthew Fort. Both chefs are gunning for success and are doing everything they can to deliver their menus to perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs compete in the heat for the central region. Returner Ryan Simson-Trotman, who failed to reach the national finals last year, is originally from Nuneaton and opened his own restaurant Orwells in the Oxfordshire countryside with his partner Liam, who is also a contestant in the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are pulling out all the stops. Marianne pays tribute to the `angels in her local hospital who looked after her during a recent illness. Ryans dish is a reference to the purported health benefits of fish high in omega 3 oils. While Sabrina is inspired by her aunt, a retired nurse who used to take in food to share with her fellow workers during her shifts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and following yesterdays results, tension is high. Both Marianne and Ryan are cooking muntjac in the hope of impressing the veteran judge, while Sabrina is making an ambitious technical take on a family feast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With one dish left to impress the veteran judge, the chefs cook their desserts. Marianne is looking to raise a smile with her `carry on themed dessert, complete with surprise presentation. Sabrina hopes to make it through with an elevated take on jelly and ice cream while Ryan goes all out with his very sweet treat `A Spoonful of Sugar.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining central region chefs do battle for a place in the national finals. They must impress the formidable panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton with their four course menus.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andrew is attempting to pay tribute to NHS staff with his innovative take on a traditional healing recipe, `Mums Flu Fighting Chicken Soup. Jasons `A Tea From The Heart is a play on a post-war remedy which harks back to the era when the NHS was formed. And Chris is hoping to impress with his dish inspired by a therapeutic hospital garden featuring foraged herbs from the Welsh valley where he lives including wild chervil, pickled pine buds and yarrow tips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After the starter results, the chefs return to the kitchen hoping for high scores with their fish dishes. Chris once again is using ingredients he has foraged from around his restaurant, while Jason hopes to impress with a spicy scallop dish and Andrew prepares a cod dish with 11 different elements.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are pulling out all the stops in a bid for big scores with their mains. All three dishes pay tribute to the founder of the NHS Aneurin Bevan, Chriss `Everything But The Squeal is an ambitious pork sharing platter, while Jasons `For The Rich And The Poor showcases lamb, and Andrew is hoping to impress with `Aneurin Bevan For Better Steak.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and the last opportunity for the chefs to impress. Chris is hoping to triumph with another dish which showcases the foraged ingredients available around his restaurant, while Jason attempts a technical feat in his dessert inspired by a childhood hospital stay and Andrew is inspired by a dish by his great grandmother used to cook. Which two will make it through to the judging chamber?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Wales regional final as the two remaining chefs do everything they can to impress the judging panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. After tensions ran high in the week, the chefs are under pressure to deliver perfection. The judges are joined by Aneira Thomas, the first baby born on the NHS, who is named in tribute to the services founder Aneurin Bevan and who went on to dedicate her life to the service. Who will win a place in the national finals?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the turn of three chefs representing the South West, including two who have competed before but meet in the kitchen this week for the very first time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and with Tom renowned as a fish specialist all eyes are on him. Hes cooking his take on a classic British fish supper in tribute to a charity which looks after the health of local fishermen. Jude is attempting a complex fusion dish featuring four different fish and an unusual X Ray themed presentation, while Olivia is paying tribute to the staff who looked after her during the birth of her first son with her take on curried monkfish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and two of the chefs are hoping duck will win the day. Tom is preparing a technically challenging duck dish steeped in NHS history while Jude is making a multi-element Chinese duck dish inspired by his mum. Olivia is hoping her dish, using two unusual cuts of beef, will score top points. Olivia also visits her local childrens hospital, home of the second busiest paediatric AandE in the country.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With just one more chance to impress their veteran judge, the chefs are all hoping to use their pastry skills to deliver technically complex desserts. Toms cooking an `Invalid Fruit Tart inspired by a 1950s recipe, while Jude is making six different apple elements for his `An Apple A Day. Olivia is using stout in reference to an outdated old wives tale which suggested the drink was a healthy source of iron during pregnancy.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its time for the two remaining chefs representing the South West to go head to head as they cook their menus again for the exacting panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. With a spot in the National Finals at stake, both are aiming for perfection as they bid for glory. The judges are joined by Barbara Childs, a Matron in the Cardiac Intensive Care Unit at Great Ormond Street Hospital, who isnt afraid to speak her mind in the chamber.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs take to the kitchen in a bid to represent the North West in the national final. They include Ellis Barrie, from the award winning Marram Grass in Anglesey, Liam Simpson-Trotman, an accomplished pastry chef and co-owner of the 4 AA rosette Orwells in Oxfordshire, and Craig Sherrington, who sharpened his skills working under top names including Eric Chavot and is now Chef Patron of Virginia House in Cumbria.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are all gunning for tens. Craig pays tribute to a friend who has revolutionised hospital food in Morecambe Bay, while Liam is inspired by health advice his grandma gave him and Ellis is dedicating his dish to the staff who delivered his son Albert. With cod, mackerel, shrimp and cockles on the menu, which of the chefs will score highly?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Time for the chefs to cook their mains and the pressure is on. Craig is hoping to impress using two cuts of Dexter beef, while Liam prepares his take on a Bajan lamb stew and Ellis prepares four cuts of suckling pig. Liam meets his personal hero, Nola Ishmael OBE, who became the UKs first ever black Director of Nursing.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the last chance for the North West chefs to impress in a bid to make it through to the regional final. Liam is cooking `Not So Hot Toddy in honour of a friend who works nightshifts in the NHS, while Ellis `Worth Their Weight in Gold is dedicated to the staff who saved his brothers life and Craigs dish `The Missed Celebration pays tribute to his wife with an unusual Christmas concept.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the week return to cook their menus again. Judging them are Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver who are joined by Dr Jake Dunning MBE, an infectious disease specialist who has been recognised for his work during the West Africa Ebola epidemic.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs are doing battle to represent Northern Ireland. Tommy Heaney, Head Chef at the Great House Hotel in South Wales is taken on by Shauna Froydenlund, Chef Patron at Marcus Warings two Michelin starred restaurant and James Devine, the 2017 National Chef of the Year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course, the chefs return to the kitchen to cook their fish dishes. James is making his elevated take on a fried chicken takeaway as a tribute to the NHS staff who care for his sister. Shauna is also taking inspiration from a personal connection with a tribute to staff from a facility in her hometown of Londonderry. Tommy is hoping to impress with a dish dedicated to the discovery of DNA.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With two courses down its time for the chefs to cook their main courses. Its the battle of beef with each using the same meat in their innovative creations. Tommy also meets the first baby ever born on the NHS.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert course and the final chance for the chefs to impress. After such a tightly fought battle all week, each is hoping to deliver a technically perfect dessert. Tommy and James are both using passion fruit in their complex dishes while Shauna is hoping to use her pastry chef training to score full marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs representing Northern Ireland go head to head. Judging as ever are Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver who are joined by Shehan Hettiaratchy, lead trauma surgeon at St Marys Hospital who helped treat victims of the Grenfell Tower fire.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs from all 8 regions must now battle it out for the honour of a spot on 2018s `Feast To Say Thank You banquet menu, celebrating 70 years of the NHS, one of Britains most prized institutions.Each day the regional champions will be cooking one of their courses again for the discerning panel of judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs from all 8 regions must now battle it out for the honour of a spot on 2018s `Feast To Say Thank You banquet menu, celebrating 70 years of the NHS, one of Britains most prized institutions.Each day the regional champions will be cooking one of their courses again for the discerning panel of judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but after some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the starter round, everyone is desperate to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. Once again the chefs present their innovative, witty or heart warming creations, with lobster, mackerel, John Dory, turbot and hake among the dishes being scrutinized.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has a hair-raising experience at a barbers in Rochdale, Lancashire, while Martin Roberts looks round a great bungalow in Alsager, Cheshire, and Lucy Alexander sees all the potential in a piece of land in Eccles in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin visit a one-bedroom flat in Catford, London, a large house near the River Mersey and a semi-detached property in Alvaston in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An end-terrace house in Alfreton in Derby bought by two brothers, a mid-terrace on the coast at Seaham in County Durham and a large three-storey property with a flat and shop in Taylorstown in the Rhondda Valley are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin talk to the new owners to find out what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts falls in love with a quirky cottage in Malvern bought by a builder and his wife, Dion Dublin talks to a first-time property developer who sees her end-terrace house in Chell in Stoke-on-Trent as something for her pension pot, and Lucy Alexander meets a divorced couple who have decided to form a new union as property developers doing up a shop and flat in Dover, Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large piece of land in Ratby in Leicestershire, a terrace in Chesterton in Staffordshire and a 1930s semi with a huge garden and huge potential are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the developers hoping to breathe new life into their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi in Sutton Coldfield near Birmingham, a derelict house in a rural village near Dover in Kent and a basement without planning permission in Ipswich in Suffolk are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 20-year-old first-time developer, a banker turned developer and a builder who wants to create a new home for his soon-to-be-wife all buy properties at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion are in Chesterton, Hornchurch and Sudbrook to hear their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A run-down listed village house in Farningham in Kent is given the wow factor while houses in Great Barr in Birmingham and Crewe in Cheshire are also given great makeovers. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and catch up on their progress after the transformations.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy talks to a pastor and his wife who plan to build their dream eco house in Brompton in Kent. Meanwhile, Martin meets a first-time developer in Wolverhampton, and Dion looks at a huge building which already comes with a tenanted flat and a hair salon in Newcastle-under-Lyme.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A commercial and residential building in Beeston in Nottinghamshire, a four-storey house in East Dulwich in London and a flat in Godalming in Surrey are all given high-end makeovers. Will the developers, who all bought at auction, make a profit?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s semi in Hatfield in Hertforshire, a flat in Croydon and a workshop and land in Heanor in Derbyshire are sold at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the owners and hear their plans to make money from their bricks and mortar.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mum and dad buy a piece of land in Stevenage in Hertfordshire in the hope of developing a pair of houses for their children, a couple who buy a top-floor flat in Motherwell in Scotland want to make a profit on the rent, and two young brothers buy a run-down house in South Normanton.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Langton Green in Kent is given a high-end refurbishment and extension, a father-and-son team cleverly refigure the existing space in a terraced house in Stoke-on-Trent, and two lifelong friends have high hopes for a piece of land they buy behind a house they already own in Northwood in Middlesex.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s semi in Leamington Spa in Warwickshire, a terraced house in Londons Plumstead and a workshop on the Isle of Sheppey in Kent are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin hear about the plans for the three properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up on ambitious plans to redevelop the site of a former school in Ebbw Vale in Wales, while Dion Dublin talks to a first-time developer planning to extend up and out at a semi in Bloxwich in the West Midlands.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Sutton in London, a studio apartment in Chatham in Kent and a terraced house in Middlesbrough are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Biggin Hill with huge potential, a centuries-old cottage in a beautiful part of Devon and an end terrace crying out for an upstairs bathroom are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell joins the team, and looks at an old mews house in an historic part of Litchfield in Staffordshire, while Martin Roberts assesses the potential of a bungalow in Hanley in Stoke-on-Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks at a large terraced house in Dover in Kent with bags of potential. Martin Roberts is in Camborne in Cornwall viewing a house with lots of issues. Dion Dublin meets a couple who bought a flat in Woodford Green in Essex.

programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Dan refuses to tell the police the truth, prompting a furious Steve to turn to Becky for comfort. Gail considers visiting Sarah in Milan when Ted offers to keep an eye on David.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve calls on Lloyd when he finds himself stranded at Roys after his night with Becky. A pent-up Carla learns Liam is coming home and Gail announces shes going to visit Sarah.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick appears to be uninterested when there is a spate of petrol thefts in the village, and Blaketon is enraged to discover that he is neglecting his job to spend time with Kate and the baby.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick struggles to combine fatherhood with full-time police work - until his mum comes to the rescue, and Greengrass sets up a darts match with high stakes.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: With the departure of Dr Dibbs, Dr Martin Ellingham moves back into his old surgery, agreeing to stay for a couple of months until a replacement can be appointed. Determined to get to grips with fatherhood, he asks Louisa to move into his house so he can help to raise their child. At the funeral of his Aunt Joan, Martin shocks the villagers when his eulogy becomes a health warning about the dangers of eating the wrong food.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Sleepless nights are taking their toll on Martin and Louisa and tempers are starting to fray. Aunt Ruth has problems of her own - a thief has stolen the hub caps from her car. The thief is identified as Michael Dunwich, who lives with his mother Shirley on a neighbouring farm. Later, Shirley tells Martin about her crippling stomach pains and reveals that she thinks her son is trying to poison her.

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: DCI Banks is drawn into the criminal underworld of Soho when he reluctantly accepts an unofficial request from Chief Superintendent Rydell to find Rydells missing daughter. With only an internet lead to go on, can Banks find Emily and persuade her to come home? Meanwhile, an armed robbery in Eastvale turns into a murder investigation after the thief is found brutally executed in his own home.

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: With the discovery of the body in the nightclub, Banks finds himself at the heart of a murder investigation with any number of suspects - and many unanswered questions. When a surveillance operation establishes a clear link between Barry Clough and the murder of Charles McKay, Banks and Cabbot realise that the two cases must be connected. Then the investigation takes a number of alarming twists.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Lewis and Hathaway investigate the death of Professor Miranda Thornton, who apparently killed herself after her dating video was leaked on to a vicious media blog. However, Lewis does not believe that Thorntons death was suicide and he has qualms about the blogs founder, Kit Renton, and the owner of the dating website, Sophie Leland. When one of Rentons employees is found murdered, the police realise she must have been killed.

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan finds that living at home cramps his love life, so he moves out.

programme.title: River Monsters

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its judgement day for the BandB owners as they discover who wins as the weeks best-value establishment. As tensions run high, it falls to final hosts Ed and Rachel to reveal the winner.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in north Kent is clairvoyant and medium Yvette Tamara. The night gets off to a spooky start when data analyst John Tower and working mum Yemi Christopher-Soares discover a ghost busting machine.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Data analyst John Tower is second to host in north Kent. John has a passion for military re-enactment and barks orders at the table. However, not all of the guests are won over by Johns Filipino fatty pork stew and psychedelic dessert.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Project manager Yemi Christopher-Soares hopes her guests in north Kent will lap up her Nigerian-inspired menu and share her fondness for spice.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the penultimate night in north Kent and the turn of cocky Jono Simm, who is convinced his wholesome and hearty menu is a winner. Sadly two of his guests arent convinced by the cooking.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final night in north Kent and the turn of cheeky party-loving Lisa Townend to be the host. Her menu is full of mysterious French fancies, including coq surprise, while her innovative entertainment is human roulette.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the Isle of Wight, were at John and Sue Blakemores Golf House. Their guests and rivals for the week are Londoners Nick Tyrie and Layla Amini, John Graham from Blackpool and hostel-owners Victoria and David Sharrock.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Nick Tyrie and his best friend Layla Amini welcome the guests to the New Inn pub in St. Johns Wood, London. Singer songwriter Layla reveals her `Top of the Pops past and treats the guests to a performance.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At the Bunkroom hostel in Chester, David Sharrock is determined to prove hostelling is for everybody - even if his wife Victoria isnt convinced. Its bunkbeds and budget breakfast for all.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit of the week is to John Grahams The B and B in Blackpool, where an afternoon of ten pin bowling finds the competitive levels reaching fever pitch.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers meet one last time to find out what theyve been paid and confront their critics face to face. Tension runs high as the payments come to light, but who will be crowned best value BandB of the week?
